mechanism_narrative: Higher federal funds rates increase borrowing costs and discount rates, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC exceeds 0.05 in the positive direction over a 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no significant negative correlation with FEDFUNDS levels.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 4.0 percent, labor market slack signals impending economic deceleration, triggering immediate negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns remain positive in >55% of observations where UNRATE exceeds 4.0, or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.02) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates robust domestic demand, driving positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon through earnings growth expectations.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative 60-day returns in >60% of cases, or the interaction term fails to improve model fit (F-test p > 0.10) over individual proxies.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a late-cycle expansion phase reflects capacity constraints and supply chain bottlenecks, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during late-cycle phases are followed by positive 20-day returns in >50% of instances, or the signal's predictive accuracy vanishes outside the specified regime.

mechanism_narrative: Rising nonfarm payrolls indicate strengthening economic activity and corporate revenue growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows no statistically significant positive correlation with 20-day forward returns (t-stat < 1.96), or Rank IC remains below 0.03 over rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the CPIAUCSL index exceeds 325, elevated inflation expectations force markets to price in aggressive central bank tightening, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in >65% of observations where CPIAUCSL exceeds 325, or the threshold effect disappears in out-of-sample validation.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in real GDP and industrial production confirms synchronized demand and supply expansion, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term yields negative 20-day returns in >55% of triggered cases, or the combined signal underperforms a univariate baseline in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained federal funds rates above 3.5 percent during a monetary tightening cycle suppresses equity valuations and risk appetite, producing negative S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns remain positive in >60% of observations during tightening cycles when FEDFUNDS exceeds 3.5, or the regime-specific signal shows no incremental predictive power.
